# OpenForge — Full Documentation for LLMs

> OpenForge is a free, open-source macOS desktop application that orchestrates parallel Claude Code agents across isolated git worktrees.

## Overview

OpenForge lets developers run multiple Claude Code AI coding agents simultaneously, each in its own git worktree and branch. One agent fixes a bug while another adds a feature while a third writes tests — all at the same time, all in isolated branches, all visible in a single native macOS window.

- License: MIT (fully open-source, not open-core, not freemium)
- Platform: macOS 12+ (Apple Silicon and Intel)
- Current version: 0.2.5
- No telemetry, no analytics, no tracking, no account required
- Tech stack: Tauri 2.x (Rust backend) + React 19 + TypeScript + Vite (frontend)

## Architecture

Three-layer architecture:

1. Rust backend (src-tauri/): Process management via PTY (portable-pty), git worktree lifecycle, checkpoint system, SQLite state persistence, script execution
2. React frontend (src/): Three-panel IDE layout, chat UI, terminal rendering (xterm.js), file viewer and diff (CodeMirror 6), Zustand state management
3. Tauri IPC bridge: Commands (frontend calls backend) and Events (backend pushes to frontend via streaming)

## Core Features

### Parallel Workspaces
Each task gets its own git worktree and branch. Agents never interfere with each other. Create a workspace with Cmd+N, pick a repo, and describe the task.

### Claude Code Integration
Each workspace gets its own Claude Code session with full multi-turn history. Supports Haiku, Sonnet, and Opus model selection. Spawns the Claude CLI with streaming NDJSON output.

### Git-Backed Checkpoints
Before every user message, OpenForge creates a git ref snapshot at refs/openforge-checkpoints/<message_id>. One-click revert rolls back to any previous state.

### Diff Viewer
Right panel shows files changed vs. base branch with color-coded diffs. Side-by-side CodeMirror merge view for comparing worktree against base branch.

### File Explorer
Browse the complete worktree file tree with syntax-highlighted preview for 15+ language grammars including JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Rust, Go, Java, HTML, CSS, JSON, YAML, Markdown, and more.

### Integrated Terminal
Full PTY-backed terminal (xterm.js) running in each worktree directory.

### Tab System
Central pane supports multiple tabs per workspace: chat sessions, file viewers, diff viewers, and changes overview.

### Workspace Naming
Workspaces are named after fictional places from literature, movies, anime, and mythology (200+ names like rivendell, tatooine, hogwarts, wakanda).

## How It Works

1. Add a repo: Select any local git repository. Branches and remotes are detected automatically.
2. Create a workspace: OpenForge runs `git worktree add` to create an isolated directory on its own branch. Runs optional setup scripts (e.g., npm install).
3. Chat with Claude: Type a prompt. OpenForge spawns `claude -p --output-format stream-json --model <model>`. NDJSON responses stream in real time. Follow-up messages use --resume --session-id for context continuity.
4. Review changes: The right panel shows live diffs, a full file tree, and an integrated terminal.
5. Ship: Merge the branch when satisfied.

## Data Storage

- Database: SQLite at ~/openforge/data/openforge.db
- Worktrees: ~/openforge/workspaces/<repo-slug>/<workspace-name>/
- Checkpoint refs: refs/openforge-checkpoints/<message-id>

## Building from Source

Prerequisites: macOS, Node.js 20+, Rust (stable), Git 2.20+, Claude Code CLI.

    git clone https://github.com/samagra14/openforge.git
    cd openforge
    npm install
    npm run tauri build
